Oracc: HBTIN — Hellenistic Babylonia: Texts, Iconography, Names

YOS 20, 079 [Sale of storehouse and second storey house][via hbtin]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[mde-e-mi-gu-ra-te-e A ša₂ ...]

(o 1) [Demokrates son of ....] sold [...] which is near the entry gate of the Reš Temple [...] the upper length to the north is adjacent to the [...] that Reš Temple; the lower length to the south is adjacent to [...] the gatekeeper of the city quarter of that Reš Temple and adjacent to the access-way [...] that bīt qāti; the upper width to the west is adjacent to the house of that man, and the courtyard of the work house of that Reš Temple; the lower width to the east is adjacent to the large partition wall which is before the street of that Reš Temple; the total of lengths and widths of the measurements of that bīt qāti and that house with a roof-room, that bīt qāti and that house with a roof-room, as little and as much as exist, all of it, for fifty shekels of refined silver, high quality staters of Demetrios, as the complete price, to Idat-Anu son of Dumqi-Anu son of Arad-Reš, who is of the priesthood of the Reš Temple, in perpetuity.

o 22

[...]

o 33

[...]-⸢x ŠEŠ? x x⸣ [...]

o 44

[...] ša₂ ina KA₂ ne₂-re-bi ša₂ e₂⸢SAG⸣ [...]

o 55

[...]-ki-ta-nu US₂ ANu₂ tu₁₅SI.⸢SA₂ DA E₂⸣ [...]

o 66

[... e₂]⸢re⸣-eš MU-MEŠ US₂ KIu₂ tu₁₅U₁₈.LU DA

o 77

[...] lu₂I₃.⸢DU₈⸣ ša₂ KA₂tu ša₂ e₂re-eš MU-MEŠ u DA mu-ṣu-u₂

o 88

[x?]-⸢x E₂⸣ ŠU-MIN MU-MEŠ SAG.KI AN.TA tu₁₅MAR.TU DA E₂ LU₂ MU-MEŠ

o 99

u TUR₃ ša₂ E₂ dul-lu ša₂ e₂SAG MU-MEŠ SAG.KI KI.TA tu₁₅KUR.RA

o 1010

DA ima-ma-aš₂-tu₄ GALti ša₂ IGI SILA ša₂ e₂re-eš MU-MEŠ ŠU.NIGIN

o 1111

US₂-MEŠ u SAG.KI-MEŠ mi-šiḫ-tu₄ E₂ ŠU-MIN u E₂ ru-gu-bu-šu₂ MU-MEŠ

o 1212

E₂ ŠU-MIN u E₂ ru-gu-bu-šu₂ MU-MEŠ i-ṣi u ma-a-du ma-la ba-šu-u₂

o 1313

gab-bi a-na 50 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR qa-lu-u₂ is-ta-tir-ra-an-nu

o 1414

ša₂ mde-e-me₂-eṭ-ri-su bab-ba-nu-u₂-tu₂ a-na ŠAM₂ TIL-MEŠ

o 1515

a-na mi-dat-d60 A ša₂ mdum-qi₂-d60 A ša₂ mARAD-e₂SAG ša₂ TA

o 1616

lu₂ki-niš-tu₄ ša₂ e₂SAG a-na u₄-mu ṣa-a-tu₂ it-tad-din KU₃.BABBAR a₄

(o 16) Demokrates, the seller of that bīt qāti and that house with a roof room, received that fifty shekels of silver, the price of that bīt qāti and that house with a roof-room, from Idat-Anu; he is paid.

o 1717

50 GIN₂ ŠAM₂ E₂ ŠU-MIN u E₂ ru-gu-bu-šu₂ MU-MEŠ TIL-MEŠ

o 1818

m⸢de⸣-e-mi-gu-ra-te-e lu₂na-din E₂ ŠU-MIN u E₂ ru-gu-bu-šu₂

Reverse
r 1r 1

MU-MEŠ ina ŠU-MIN mi-dat-d60 ma-ḫir e-ṭir₃ u₄-mu pa-qa-ri a-na

(r 1) Should a claim concerning that bīt qāti, that house with a roof-room, and that access-way arise, Demokrates, the seller of that bīt qāti and that house with a roof-room, will clear it of claims, and will pay the 12-fold penalty to Idat-Anu son of Dumqi-Anu in perpetuity.

r 22

muḫ-ḫi E₂ ŠU-MIN E₂ ru-gu-bu-šu₂ u mu-ṣu-u₂-šu₂ MU-MEŠ

r 33

it-tab-šu-ma mde-e-mi-gu-ra-te-e lu₂na-din E₂ ŠU-MIN

r 44

u E₂ ru-gu-bu-šu₂ MU-MEŠ u₂-mar-raq-qa-ma a-di 12-TA-AM₃

r 55

a-na mi-dat-d60 A ša₂ mdum-qi₂-d60 a-na u₄-mu ṣa-a-tu₂ ina-an-din

r 66

E₂ ŠU-MIN E₂ ru-gu-bu-šu₂ u mu-ṣu-u₂-šu₂ MU-MEŠ ša₂ mi-dat-d60

(r 6) That bīt qāti, that house with a roof-room, and that access-way belong to Idat-Anu son of Dumqi-Anu son of Arad-Reš, who is of the priesthood of the Reš Temple, in perpetuity.

r 77

A ša₂ mdum-qi₂-d60 A ša₂ mARAD-e₂SAG ša₂ TA lu₂ki-niš-tu₄

r 88

ša₂ e₂re-eš a-na u₄-mu ṣa-a-tu₂ šu-u₂

(witnesses)
r 99

lu₂mu-kin₇

(r 9) Witnesses: Kidin-Anu son of Anu-belšunu son of Anu-ahhe-iddin and Anu-belšunu son of Nidintu-Anu son of Anu-belšunu, descendants of Ah'utu.

r 1010

mBARA₂-d60 A ša₂ md60-EN-šu₂-nu A ša₂ md60-ŠEŠ-MEŠ-MU u md60-EN-šu₂-nu

r 1111

A ša₂ mNIG₂.SUM.MU-d60 A ša₂ md60-EN-šu₂-nu A-MEŠ mŠEŠ-ʾu-u₂-tu₂

r 1212

mina-qi₂-bit-d60 u md60-AD-URI₃ A ša₂ md60-AD-URI₃ A ša₂ md60-DUMU-MU[nu]

(r 12) Ina-qibit-Anu and Anu-ab-uṣur, sons of Anu-ab-uṣur, son of Anu-mar-ittannu descendant of Hunzu; Anu-zer-iddin son of Anu-ab-uṣur son of Anu-zer-iddin and Anu-mar-ittannu son of Kidin-Anu son of Anu-zer-iddin descendants of Ekur-zakir

r 1313

A mḫun-zu-u₂ md60-NUMUN-MU A ša₂ md60-AD-URI₃ A ša₂ md60-NUMUN-⸢MU⸣

r 1414

u md60-DUMU-MUnu A ša₂ mBARA₂-d60 A ša₂ md60-NUMUN-MU A-MEŠ mE₂.[KUR-za-kir]

r 1515

mtat-tan-nu A ša₂ md60-ŠEŠ-MU A ⸢ša₂ mri⸣-ḫat-d60 A m⸢x⸣-[...]

(r 15) Tattannu son of Anu-ah-iddin son of Rihat-Anu descendant of [...]; Anu-balassu-iqbi son of Ana-rabut-Anu son of [Anu]-eriba descendant of [Kuri]

r 1616

md60-DIN-su-E A ša₂ mana-GAL-d60 [A ša₂ md60]-SU A ⸢m⸣[...]

(colophon)
r 1717

md60-DINiṭ lu₂UMBISAG A ša₂ m[...] A mE₂.[KUR-za-kir]

(r 17) Anu-uballiṭ, scribe, son of [...] descendant of Ekur-zakir. (Written in) Uruk. 24th(?) day of Ayyaru [year ...]

r 1818

UNUGki itiGU₄-KAM₂ U₄ 24-[x?]-⸢KAM₂⸣ [...]

Column i
r.e. i 1r.e. i 1

[un-qa mde-e-mi]-gu-ra-te-e

(r.e. i 1) [Ring of] Demokrates, seller of that bīt qāti and that house with a roof-room

(impression of seal = AUWE 19, 0259)
r.e. i 22

[lu₂na-din] E₂ ŠU-MIN u E₂ ru-gu-bu-šu₂ MU-MEŠ
